sections.instruction: Complete the sentences below, choose answers from the list A-F, and choose THREE letters A-H.

questions.content: (21) Phil and Stella’s goal is to {{BLANK}} the hypothesis that weather has an effect on a person’s mood. (22) They expect to find that ‘good’ weather (weather which is {{BLANK}} and {{BLANK}}) has a positive effect on a person’s mood. (23) Stella defines ‘effect on mood’ as a {{BLANK}} in the way a person feels.
What information was given by each writer?
options: A the benefits of moving to a warmer environment
B the type of weather with the worst affect on mood
C how past events affect attitudes to weather
D the important effect of stress on mood
E the importance of effect of hours of sunshine on modd
F psychological problems due to having to cope with bad weather

questions.content: Which THREE things do Phil and Stella still have to decide on?
options: A how to analyse their results
B their methods of presentation
C the design of their questionnaire
D the location of their survey
E weather variables to be measured
F the dates of their survey
G the size of their survey
H the source of data on weather variables

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B or C, and complete the summary below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

questions.content: How long would terms be under the six-term system?
options: A six weeks
B seven weeks
C thirteen weeks

questions.content: What would happen to the summer holiday?
options: A it would disappear
B it would be shortened
C it would be lengthened

questions.content: How much was the average learning loss in the summer?
options: A two weeks
B three weeks
C seven weeks

questions.content: In which subject was learning loss greatest among disadvantaged?
options: A maths
B reading
C writing

questions.content: According to Marchmont’s research, in the six-term system pupils performed
options: A better than under the existing system
B worse than under the existing system
C the same as under the existing system
